available_to: ALL
description: Share verified situation updates, emerging risks, and resource status with other teams. Helps coordination and reduces duplicated efforts.

description: Coordinate ambulance, van, and volunteer transport for dialysis patients and other critical cases across county lines. Establishes routing, scheduling, and priority system for medically fragile residents.

available_to: Carilion Health System Crisis Command;SW Virginia Mass Casualty Coordination
description: Request and deploy mobile dialysis units to Wise and Buchanan counties. Requires state authorization and coordination with regional hospital networks. Treats patients in place rather than requiring transport.

available_to: Carilion Health System Crisis Command;SW Virginia Mass Casualty Coordination
description: Activate formal mutual aid agreements to transfer critical patients (dialysis, ICU, respiratory) to Carilion Roanoke Memorial and other regional hospitals with available capacity. Requires bed coordination and transport planning.

available_to: SW Virginia Mass Casualty Coordination;Governor's Health Emergency Coordinator
description: Issue state authorization for non-emergency medical transport across county lines, enabling broader use of volunteer and community vehicles for medical appointments. Reduces friction for patient access.

available_to: Shentel Emergency Health Communications;Carilion Health System Crisis Command
description: Reserve and prioritize network bandwidth for telehealth consultations at rural clinics and community health sites. Enables remote dialysis monitoring, MOUD consultations, and respiratory follow-up without in-person transport.

available_to: AEP Power Grid & Environmental Systems;VA DEQ Emergency Environmental Response
description: Deploy emergency geotechnical crews to reinforce the eastern berm of the Clinch River coal ash pond before the rainfall event. Expensive and time-sensitive — effective only if initiated before storm arrives. Reduces breach risk significantly.

available_to: VA DEQ Emergency Environmental Response;Virginia Tech Environmental Health Research
description: Commission rapid structural engineering assessment of the coal ash pond berm to determine breach probability, timeline, and recommended containment actions. Informs the stabilization vs. advisory decision.

available_to: VA DEQ Emergency Environmental Response;Montgomery County Public Health Authority;WDBJ7 Health & Environmental Coverage
description: Issue a precautionary boil-water advisory for communities served by the three affected rural water systems. Legally defensible precaution — but triggers mandatory bottled water distribution and can cause panic if messaging is poor.

available_to: SW Virginia Mass Casualty Coordination;Regional Medical & Food Supply Chain
description: Procure and distribute bottled water to communities under a water advisory. Requires logistics coordination, community distribution sites, and prioritization of medically vulnerable households.

available_to: VA DEQ Emergency Environmental Response;Virginia Tech Environmental Health Research
description: Send water samples to state lab for accelerated confirmatory testing on arsenic and lead findings. Results available in 24-48 hours. Required to confirm or retract preliminary results and establish enforcement basis.

available_to: Governor's Health Emergency Coordinator;SW Virginia Mass Casualty Coordination
description: Declare a regional public health emergency. Unlocks emergency funding, activates mutual aid agreements, authorizes emergency pharmaceutical dispensing, and triggers FEMA eligibility assessment for public health response costs.

available_to: Carilion Health System Crisis Command;Governor's Health Emergency Coordinator
description: Coordinate emergency MOUD pharmaceutical resupply from state stockpile and regional distributors. Requires state authorization and cold chain logistics. Addresses root cause of MOUD clinic suspension.

available_to: Carilion Health System Crisis Command;SW Virginia Mass Casualty Coordination
description: Activate emergency take-home medication extensions for MOUD patients — 2-week supply instead of daily dispensing. Reduces clinic visit burden while maintaining treatment continuity. Requires DEA emergency authorization.

available_to: Carilion Health System Crisis Command;United Way Health Navigation Services;SW Virginia Sheriff's Environmental Crime Unit
description: Deploy a rapid-response team with naloxone, counselors, and peer recovery specialists to high-risk areas. Reduces overdose fatalities during supply crisis and connects patients to emergency medication alternatives.

available_to: United Way Health Navigation Services;Appalachian Faith Network Community Health
description: Deploy trained lay health workers to opioid recovery communities, dialysis patient households, and rural hollows to assess needs, provide information, and connect residents to emergency services.

available_to: United Way Health Navigation Services;Governor's Health Emergency Coordinator
description: Provide emergency bridge funding to MOUD clinics facing supply crisis to maintain operations while pharmaceutical resupply is arranged. Prevents suspension and keeps clinic staff engaged.

available_to: VA DEQ Emergency Environmental Response;Virginia Tech Environmental Health Research
description: Deploy rapid-deployment air quality sensors in communities surrounding the coal processing facility and coal ash pond. Provides real-time particulate and chemical data to support respiratory illness investigation and public communication.

available_to: VA DEQ Emergency Environmental Response;AEP Power Grid & Environmental Systems
description: Order a mandatory environmental safety review of the coal processing facility restart — including emissions monitoring protocols, ash pond status, and worker safety certification. Pauses operations until review is complete. Requires Governor's authorization.

available_to: Virginia Tech Environmental Health Research;VA DEQ Emergency Environmental Response
description: Launch an epidemiological investigation of the Appalachia respiratory cluster, including case interviews, environmental sample collection, and laboratory analysis. Produces a preliminary cause assessment within 48 hours.

available_to: Public Health Biosecurity Coordinator;Governor's Health Emergency Coordinator
description: Submit formal CDC emergency consultation request for the respiratory cluster. CDC provides technical support, additional laboratory capacity, and federal guidance on whether biosecurity escalation criteria are met.

available_to: VA DEQ Emergency Environmental Response;Montgomery County Public Health Authority
description: Host transparent public forums in affected coal communities to explain environmental risks, share what is known and unknown, and receive community input. Reduces conspiracy traction and builds trust for difficult decisions.

available_to: Public Health Biosecurity Coordinator;Virginia Tech Environmental Health Research
description: Conduct a formal assessment of the respiratory cluster against biosecurity escalation criteria — including unusual presentation patterns, geographic spread, and cyber attack context. Produces a classified/unclassified summary for decision-makers.

available_to: VA DEQ Emergency Environmental Response;SW Virginia Sheriff's Environmental Crime Unit
description: Issue enforcement orders based on environmental findings — including potential halt of coal operations, mandatory reporting requirements, and fines. Requires Governor authorization. Effective but risks political backlash in coal-dependent communities.

available_to: WDBJ7 Health & Environmental Coverage;SW Virginia Mass Casualty Coordination
description: Stand up a coordinated health communications cadence — regular updates on water advisory status, dialysis transport availability, MOUD clinic status, and respiratory investigation progress. Reduces panic and counters misinformation.

available_to: United Way Health Navigation Services;Appalachian Faith Network Community Health
description: Conduct door-to-door and phone outreach to elderly, homebound, and medically fragile residents in hollows and remote areas. Identifies unmet needs and connects individuals to emergency services before conditions become critical.

available_to: Governor's Health Emergency Coordinator
description: Request National Guard field medical units to supplement Carilion's strained capacity. Provides additional medical personnel, mobile clinics, and logistics support for mass casualty scenarios. Requires federal coordination.

available_to: Carilion Health System Crisis Command;SW Virginia Mass Casualty Coordination;Regional Medical & Food Supply Chain
description: Stand up emergency pharmacy dispensing at community centers, churches, and schools in underserved areas. Provides temporary access to MOUD medications, insulin, and critical prescription drugs where pharmacies are closed or supply-depleted.

available_to: VA DEQ Emergency Environmental Response;WDBJ7 Health & Environmental Coverage
description: Publish a clear, plain-language environmental health status report: water system status, coal ash assessment status, air quality readings, and respiratory cluster investigation progress. Builds credibility but requires careful framing to avoid panic.

available_to: VA DEQ Emergency Environmental Response;Virginia Tech Environmental Health Research;AEP Power Grid & Environmental Systems
description: Establish a binding multi-party agreement for community-based environmental monitoring — including coal ash pond, water systems, and air quality — with public dashboard reporting. Addresses root cause of monitoring gaps that created Phase 5's hidden damage.
